member.name: Damian Hinds
member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Damian Hinds is the Conservative MP for East Hampshire , and has been an MP continually since 6 May 2010.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Youth Services: Expenditure
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what estimate she has made of her Department’s (a) capital and (b) revenue spending across youth facilities and activities programmes in (i) 2023/4, (ii) 2024/5, (iii) 2025/6 and (iv) 2026/7.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The following amounts of capital and revenue were spent on the Department’s youth facilities and activities programme between 2023/24 and 2025/26. Details of 2026/7 spending will be published in due course. In 2023/4, spending was £119.6m (£42.3m capital ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Children: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps her Department has taken with the Information Commissioner’s Office on the development of an Age Appropriate Design code for Artificial Intelligence applications.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department is clear that, while we recognise the transformative impact that artificial intelligence (AI) and digital technologies can have on education, they can only be effective when implemented and used safely. Departmental officials are engaged wi...

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs: Neurodiversity
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, how the Partnerships for inclusion of neurodiversity in schools (PINS) programme and Experts at Hand programme relate to one another.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Experts at Hand is receiving significant investment of £1.8 billion over the next three years, to improve earlier support for children and young people. As local areas develop their Expert at Hand offers, they are encouraged to learn from what works and e...

writtenQuestions.heading: Payments: Fraud
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the impact of powers in The Payment Services (Amendment) Regulations 2024 on preventing, disrupting and detecting fraud.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government takes the issue of fraud very seriously and is dedicated to protecting the public from this appalling crime. I refer the hon. member to the answers given to UIN 6733 and 6734 on the 11th June 2026.

writtenQuestions.heading: Small Businesses: Loans
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, further to the Answer of 11 June 2026 to Question 7122, if he will collect data on the average time for SMEs to receive a decision on bank loan applications.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Business and Trade does not currently have plans to collect information on trends in the average time for SMEs to receive a decision on bank loan applications.

writtenQuestions.heading: Education: ICT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, how many (a) primary schools, (b) secondary schools and (c) further education colleges are participating in the next EdTech and AI pilot scheme announced in January 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department did not implement a nine-month EdTech Impact Testbed pilot in 2025. This is now a four-year national EdTech Testbed Programme which will start testing in schools next academic year.

writtenQuestions.heading: Education: ICT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, if she will publish the evidence of improved outcomes for pupils who took part in the nine-month EdTech Impact Testbed pilot in 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department did not implement a nine-month EdTech Impact Testbed pilot in 2025. This is now a four-year national EdTech Testbed Programme which will start testing in schools next academic year.

writtenQuestions.heading: Education: ICT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the outcome of the nine-month EdTech Impact Testbed pilot rolled out in 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department did not implement a nine-month EdTech Impact Testbed pilot in 2025. This is now a four-year national EdTech Testbed Programme which will start testing in schools next academic year.

writtenQuestions.heading: Pupils: ICT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what data her Department holds on how much time (a) primary and (b) secondary school children are spending on screens during (i) the school day (ii) for homework.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department’s Technology in Schools Survey 2024/25 presents data on the proportion of lessons in which pupils in primary and secondary schools used end-user devices, including desktop computers, laptops, tablets and smartphones, as well as the use of t...

writtenQuestions.heading: Primary Education: Physical Education and Sports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what estimate she has made of (a) total and (b) per pupil funding from her Department to primary schools earmarked for PE and sport (i) in AY2024/5 (ii) in AY2025/6 and (iii) projected for AY2026/7.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department is increasing investment in PE and school sport, having announced over £1 billion investment over the next three years. This will fund new PE and Sports Partnerships, capital investment in facilities and specific programmes supporting inclu...

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs: Reform
